assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: The Security Configuration section in claude-md-result.md (lines 42-64) is byte-identical to the fixture. Product Lifecycle contains all 5 fields (Product pages URL, Jira version prefix, Vulnerability issue type ID, Component label pattern, VEX Justification custom field), Version Streams has the 2.1.x row, and Source Repositories lists backend and frontend-ui — all matching the fixture exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 118-125) states: '## Security Configuration already exists with all required fields fully populated (no {{placeholder}} markers)' and concludes 'Result: **Security Configuration is up to date**.' Step 6 (line 95) also lists 'Security Configuration (including Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, Source Repositories)' as fully configured.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md shows Security Configuration status as 'Up to date' with action 'No changes' in the Sections Reviewed table (line 19). The Details section (lines 41-45) states: 'Fully populated with no placeholder markers' and lists all sub-sections. The summary (line 5) says 'Project Configuration is up to date — no changes needed.' and 'Files Modified: None' (lines 48-49).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 118-125) shows the skill detected the existing Security Configuration section with all fields populated and marked it 'up to date' without any opt-in prompt. changes-log.md confirms 'No changes' for Security Configuration. There is no evidence of any opt-in prompt being presented in any output file.

assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 5-8 show the Repository Registry table with both entries preserved exactly: 'backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/backend' and 'frontend-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/frontend-ui'. diff against the fixture shows zero differences in this section.
